# Build the Supernote Cloud Page Organizer with Speedrift and testing loops, after updating Speedrift tooling. Spec - Drift Anchor

*Generated by PlanForge V2 on 2026-06-08. Session: `20260608-supernote-organizer`. This is a drift anchor, not a standalone product brief.*

## End State

Implement the first Speedrift execution slice of the Supernote Cloud Page Organizer: read-only snapshot/browser foundation plus tested metadata-aware reorder groundwork, preserving the approved cloud-side design constraints.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Backend can build a NotebookSnapshot from .note bytes keyed by page_id.
- Snapshot exposes page order, page records, stars, headings/titles, keywords, links, and image dimensions without mutating notebooks.
- Backend has tested image rendering/cache primitives suitable for a page grid.
- A minimal local web UI can display a notebook page grid with zoom and metadata filters.
- Same-notebook reorder engine exists behind tests but blocks unsafe metadata rather than risking lossy writes.
- All implementation tasks run Speedrift drift checks and focused pytest/UX checks.

## Out Of Scope

- Cross-notebook move execution.
- Device plugin implementation.
- Partner App local sync writes.
- Custom shape or sticker marker detection.
- Automatic merge of stale cloud snapshots.

## Speedrift Modules

- coredrift (required): Baseline task contract and drift-control lane for every task.
- specdrift (required): The implementation is anchored to the approved design spec.
- datadrift (required): Snapshot, metadata index, operation, and API contracts are structured data surfaces.
- uxdrift (recommended): The project includes a web UI for grid, zoom, filters, drag states, and apply flow.
- yagnidrift (recommended): The design deliberately defers cross-note moves and custom markers; guard against overbuilding.
- fixdrift (recommended): Testing loops should create explicit repair tasks when failures exceed the current task scope.

## Verification

- snapshot tests: `uv run pytest tests/test_note_snapshot.py -q` (per_task)
- page ops regression: `uv run pytest tests/test_note_page_ops.py tests/test_note_snapshot.py -q` (per_task)
- organizer API tests: `uv run pytest tests/test_organizer_api.py -q` (per_task)
- reorder tests: `uv run pytest tests/test_note_reorder.py -q` (per_task)

## Risks

- Supernote link metadata may need more than page-number remapping. [high]: Block write-capable reorder for unsupported link metadata until fixture and device validation prove safe remapping.
- supernotelib reconstruction may omit or normalize metadata in ways that only appear on device. [medium]: Keep Phase 1 read-only and add golden fixtures before writes.
- The repository currently has unrelated uncommitted changes. [medium]: Scope commits carefully and avoid touching unrelated dirty files unless required.
